display.purpose: Page-level section headings (Get in Touch, Who We Are).

headline.purpose: Card and component titles, e.g. service names.

label.purpose: Nav links, form labels, button text.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Navbar 'Get a Quote' CTA. Deep Teal at rest, Electric Mint on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #0A4E46; color: #FFFFFF; padding: 10px 24px; border-radius: 4px; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.875rem; border: none; transition: background 0.2s, color 0.2s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #15EAC9; color: #0A1F1C; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #15EAC9; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: CTA Button
components.description: Contact form submit button. Electric Mint fill, fades to white on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-cta { background: #15EAC9; color: #0A1F1C; padding: 12px 24px; min-height: 44px; width: 100%; border-radius: 4px; font-weight: 700; border: none; transition: background 0.2s; } .ds-btn-cta:hover { background: #FFFFFF; } .ds-btn-cta:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #0A4E46; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Form field used in ContactSection. White bg, mint focus ring.
components.css: .ds-field { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; } .ds-label { color: #FFFFFF; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.875rem; } .ds-input { background: #FFFFFF; border: 1px solid #E1E2E4; border-radius: 4px; padding: 10px 12px; font-size: 1rem; color: #0A1F1C; } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #15EAC9; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #15EAC9; }

components.name: Icon Badge
components.description: Circular mint-tinted icon badge used beside phone/email/location rows.
components.css: .ds-icon-badge { width: 36px; height: 36px; border-radius: 9999px; background: rgba(21,234,201,0.15); border: 1px solid #15EAC9; color: #15EAC9; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; flex-shrink: 0; }

components.name: Contact Panel
components.description: Dark bordered panel used for contact info and form on Contact/Services/Work/About pages.
components.css: .ds-card-contact { background: #0A4E46; border: 2px solid rgba(21,234,201,0.4); border-radius: 8px; padding: 32px; color: #FFFFFF; }

components.name: Service Card
components.description: Services grid card: photo header band with teal scrim, white bold title, body + bullets beneath.
components.html:
Roofer

General roofing and repairs across Exeter and Devon.
components.css: .ds-service-card { border-radius: 8px; overflow: hidden; background: #FFFFFF; } .ds-service-card-header { position: relative; padding: 24px; background: rgba(10,78,70,0.8); display: flex; align-items: center; min-height: 96px; } .ds-service-card-title { color: #FFFFFF; font-weight: 800; font-size: 1.75rem; letter-spacing: -0.01em; } .ds-service-card-body { padding: 20px 24px; color: #191C1E; font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.6; }

components.name: Footer Logo Banner
components.description: Centered logo-led footer header, the signature departure from a standard 4-column footer.
components.html: For all aspects of roofing
components.css: .ds-footer-banner { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 12px; padding: 40px 0; border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.15); } .ds-footer-logo { width: 240px; height: auto; } .ds-footer-tagline { color: rgba(255,255,255,0.8); font-size: 0.875rem; }

narrative.overview: LA Roofing's interface should feel like the company itself: a working trade yard, not a tech showroom. Deep teal reads like powder-coated sheet metal and ridge cladding; near-black grounds the page like asphalt and steel; Electric Mint is used the way a hi-vis strip is used on site gear, a flash of attention on exactly the thing that needs it, never as wallpaper. Surfaces stay flat and bordered rather than soft and shadowed, because this is a sturdy, no-nonsense local trade business in Exeter and Devon, not a SaaS startup. Every section should earn trust through real photos of real jobs and real reviews, not through abstraction, gradients, or decorative flourish.
keyCharacteristics: Deep, saturated teal as the dominant brand color, not a pale tint of it
Electric Mint reserved for accents: borders, icon badges, primary CTAs — never as text on white
Flat surfaces with borders for definition, not soft drop shadows
Bold, heavy display type (Montserrat 800) for hierarchy, not size alone
Real business photography over illustration or iconography wherever possible

rules.name: The Hi-Vis Rule
rules.body: Electric Mint is a flash of attention, not a fill color. It appears as a border, an icon badge, or a CTA button — never as a body background and never as text directly on white or Surface.

rules.name: The Heavy-or-Quiet Rule
rules.body: Headings are always 800-weight Montserrat; body copy is always 400-weight Inter. There is no mid-weight no-man's-land between them.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces sit at one level. Definition comes from a border or a solid color change, never from box-shadow.

dos: Do use Deep Teal (#0A4E46) and Near-Black Trust (#0A1F1C) as the dominant surface colors on brand-heavy sections (contact, footer).
Do use Electric Mint (#15EAC9) only as a border, icon badge, button fill, or focus ring — never as text directly on white/Surface.
Do define depth with borders and solid color blocks, never box-shadow.
Do use real business photography as the proof mechanism wherever a section needs visual weight.
Do keep headings at Montserrat 800 and body copy at Inter 400.
Do enforce ≥44px touch targets and prefers-reduced-motion fallbacks on every interactive element and animation.
donts: Don't pair a 1px+ border with a soft wide box-shadow on the same element (the ghost-card pattern).
Don't add a tiny uppercase tracked eyebrow above every section heading.
Don't build identical icon-card grids as the default answer for any list-of-things section.
Don't use gradient text or glassmorphism decoration anywhere on the site.
Don't round cards or sections beyond 8px; full-pill rounding is reserved for chips/pills only.
Don't let Electric Mint exceed roughly 10% of any given section's surface area.
